prompt:
Re-land the content of the orphaned PR #1 ('Reframe the-valley as a recursive, transparent outcome-production engine') against the freshly restructured corpus, at the correct altitudes and thinness. PR #1 predates two doc restructures and is unmergeable as a diff; its IDEAS have been reviewed by the owner and get re-landed per the dispositions below. First: read the current corpus on main (README.md and every file in design/ — note the new layering: README=premise, requirements.md, user-scenarios.md = the requirements-layer scenario ladder, architecture.md, then slim design docs). Then read PR #1's original content with `gh pr diff 1` and `gh api 'repos/gunk-dev/the-valley/contents/design/outcomes.md?ref=<pr1-head-branch>'` etc. (find the head branch via `gh pr view 1 --json headRefName`). MINE the original prose — the owner wrote/approved it; adapt it, don't reinvent it.

## Owner dispositions (decided, do not relitigate)

**1. Premise: SDLC hook stays; generality lands BELOW it.** Do NOT rewrite the README's opening pitch. Add a short section after 'The promise' (title like 'Where this goes'): the-valley is, at its most general, a recursive, transparent outcome-production engine — outcomes chain and recurse on a dependency graph ('add code to a VCS' serves 'deliver a feature users love'), and the SDLC is the v1 REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION, not the boundary. Recursive includes: the-valley builds the-valley. Three or four sentences, zero mechanisms, keep the hook's register. The reader should finish the README understanding both the concrete v1 and the bigger shape.

**2. Thread 1 (outcome DAG) is ADOPTED as an architecture bet — but stays bet-thin.** Add a section to design/architecture.md: the knowledge graph read GENERATIVELY — 'outcome' becomes the central node type; blocked_by/blocks edges form a dependency DAG; root outcomes carry priority that propagates; the system dispatches the unblocked frontier on the critical path toward the highest-priority root (klaus-shaped dispatch). Note it splits the old priority-layer question in two: work-scheduling (this bet addresses it) vs attention-routing (still open). Trace it to the requirement it serves and anchor it to ladder rungs S3/S4. Rationale only — NO scheduler mechanics, no node-format specifics; the deleted-in-#3 detail returns when its rung is top-priority. Do NOT resurrect outcomes.md as a standalone doc.
Ripples (keep light): in design/user-scenarios.md, adjust the knowledge-increment column and S1's 'Knowledge v0' bullet so 'outcome' (oc-*) is named among the v0 node types (issues/outcomes/ideas/decisions) and S3/S4's increments read naturally against the generative-DAG bet — do not restructure the ladder. In design/requirements.md, if the project-knowledge need's wording benefits from one clause about outcomes-as-work, add it; otherwise leave it.

**3. Thread 2 (federation): ONE paragraph in architecture.md, not a doc.** The group is the unit of federation; each group runs one instance (hosting + coordination); instantiable on one machine, distributable later. Anchor to rungs S5+/S7 and note it reframes the cross-repo open questions as intra-group vs inter-group. Do NOT re-create federation.md.

**4. Thread 3 (self-transparency): re-land as the short marked-DRAFT stub + an open question.** Re-create design/self-transparency.md as a SHORT stub (roughly its original ~40 lines or less), clearly marked DRAFT / candidate invariant, deliberately unresolved: 'no actor can durably change the system or an output without transparency', recursive all the way down; collect the facets it unifies (integrator self-integration, policy bootstrap, load-bearing principles) as instances. Add a matching [requirements]-or-[architecture]-tagged entry to design/openquestions.md with origin pointer. Link it from the README doc map or architecture.md, whichever fits the layering better.

**5. openquestions.md:** apply PR #1's question updates that still make sense post-prune (the priority-layer split from Thread 1; the intra/inter-group reframe of the surviving cross-repo questions; the self-transparency entry). Match the existing layer-tag format.

## Constraints
- Respect the altitude rules and the prune bias: net additions should be SMALL (a README section, one-to-two architecture sections, a stub doc, ladder touch-ups, openquestions entries — not 200 lines of restored prose).
- All relative links resolve. Match voice.
- PR description: state this supersedes PR #1, map each PR-#1 thread to where (and how thinly) it landed, and note explicitly what was deliberately NOT restored (outcomes.md and federation.md as standalone docs — they return when their rungs are top-priority). No AI/assistant attribution, no Co-Authored-By.

Open a PR.
